title: The Bruins’ second line isn’t just a pleasant surprise. They’ve been one of the NHL’s best this year.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins' second line of Mittelstadt, Zacha, and Arvidsson has outscored opponents 35-16 this season.
This line is second only to Colorado's top line in goal differential, boasting a +19 mark in 472 minutes of 5-on-5 play.
Arvidsson and Mittelstadt have revitalized their careers in Boston, with Zacha scoring a career-high 23 goals this season.

title: He’s running Boston to support kids and families at MGH
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Andrew Mahr is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for pediatric cancer care at Massachusetts General Hospital.
Mahr first ran in 2014, inspired by young patients' courage at Heartbreak Hill and the events of the 2013 bombing.
Pediatric cancer care receives less funding than adult cancer research, motivating Mahr's fundraising efforts.

title: ‘More money in my pocket is a no-brainer’: Readers weigh in on proposed income tax cut
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: A proposed Massachusetts tax cut could reduce the income tax rate from 5% to 4%, impacting state revenue by $5.1 billion annually.
A Boston.com poll shows 78% of readers support the tax cut proposal, while 20% oppose it, citing potential service cuts.
Governor Maura Healey opposes the tax cut, saying it could harm state finances, while supporters argue it reduces unnecessary spending.

title: A voodoo doll, a love triangle, and an alleged poisoning: Testimony begins in Salisbury antifreeze murder trial
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Judy Church is on trial for first-degree murder, accused of poisoning her boyfriend Leroy Fowler with antifreeze in 2022.
Prosecution alleges Church's threats and joking about poisoning Fowler were serious, supported by evidence of antifreeze found in their home.
Defense argues Church's remarks were jokes and explains her actions as concern for Fowler's substance abuse, not intent to murder.

title: Healey pushes to get rid of Sunday hunting ban
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey files bill to end Massachusetts' ban on Sunday hunting, easing restrictions on crossbows and bowhunting setbacks.
MassWildlife supports eliminating the ban to manage deer overpopulation, with 70% of public comments backing the change.
The proposal would reduce bowhunting setback from 500 to 250 feet, opening more land for hunting in Massachusetts.

title: Cape Cod magnet fisher pulls ‘pristine-looking’ derringer pistol from Bridgewater river
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Nate DeMontigny found a pristine-looking derringer pistol inside a case while magnet fishing in a Bridgewater river.
The derringer, dated back to the 19th century, was featured in DeMontigny's YouTube video, gaining over 1,700 views.
DeMontigny immediately contacted police upon discovery; the find attracted 1.2 million views on a magnet fishing subreddit.

title: Teen charged with murder after stabbing in Chelsea
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: An 18-year-old, Sergio Josue Castellanos, is charged with murder after a fatal stabbing in Chelsea.
The incident occurred near Chestnut Street and Everett Avenue at 6:45 p.m., with multiple victims reported.
A 25-year-old man died from multiple stab wounds after surgery; other victims' details are undisclosed.

title: Two officers injured in struggle with knife-wielding man at Forest Hills station, police say
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Two officers were injured during a struggle with a knife-wielding man at Forest Hills MBTA station.
The suspect attempted to stab bus tires and reached for an officer's weapon, which discharged during the struggle.
The incident led to a temporary closure of the upper busway; regular service resumed by 1:30 p.m.

title: Man accused of kicking dog in Roxbury before being assaulted by group
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A man in Roxbury is charged with animal cruelty after allegedly kicking a dog into a street.
The incident occurred near 559 Dudley St., where a woman couldn't locate her dog, Biscuit.
Five unidentified individuals allegedly assaulted the suspect shortly after the dog incident.

title: Mass. man charged with threatening FBI director’s girlfriend
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Alden Welch Ruml, 26, of Cambridge, charged with threatening FBI Director Kash Patel's girlfriend via email.
The email suggested harm to Alexis Wilkins, citing dislike of her conservative views and security details.
Ruml, pleading not guilty, faces up to five years if convicted of making interstate threats.
